article.headline: Gabriel Martinelli scores 96th minute winner for Brazil to knock Japan out of World Cup
article.description: Gabriel Martinelli scored late injury time to give Brazil a 2-1 win over Japan in the round of 32 at the World Cup on Monday.
article.linkText: Brazil's Martinelli scores late winner to knock Japan out of World Cup

images.name: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan
images.caption: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan

video.headline: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan
video.shortHeadline: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan
video.caption: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan
video.title: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan
video.description: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan
video.shortDescription: How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan

tracking.trackingName: COM_SOC_Misc (How Brazil survived their Round of 32 clash against Japan) 2026/06/29

article.story:
Substitute Gabriel Martinelli scored a stoppage-time winner as Brazil snatched a place in the round of 16 of the World Cup with a 2-1 win over Japan in Houston.

Carlo Ancelotti's side were stunned when Japan took the lead with a superb strike from Kaishu Sano after 29 minutes but replied when Casemiro atoned for a poor first-half performance with a fine header.

The game appeared to be heading towards extra time after a tense final period but Martinelli, sent on by Ancelotti on 66 minutes, capped the fightback after being played in by Bruno Guimarães.

It was harsh on an obstinate Japan side, who were chasing their country's first World Cup knockout win, but Brazil finished strongly after a lethargic first-half display.

Japan were measured throughout, sitting deep and allowing Brazil to control possession early on.

The South Americans found it difficult to create openings but did have an early chance as a Guimaraes shot was deflected behind and Matheus Cunha had an effort pushed wide by Zion Suzuki.

Japan's only opportunity in the opening stages came from a free-kick after Junya Ito was felled by Casemiro but Daichi Kamada fired into the wall.

Sano, booked early on after catching Vinícius Júnior late, escaped further punishment for a clumsy challenge on Cunha.

That was to prove controversial as Sano gave Japan the lead just before the half-hour.

The Mainz midfielder seized on Danilo's loose pass on halfway and powered past Casemiro before burying a low shot beyond Alisson Becker from outside the box.

Brazil were rocked and initially reduced to tame long-range efforts from Vinícius and Cunha in response, but Ancelotti's half-time adjustments prompted a change of tempo.

Guimaraes tested Suzuki with a strong header and Casemiro had an even better chance moments later but planted his header against Takehiro Tomiyasu on the line when he seemed certain to score.

Ito's clearance led to a quick Japan counter-attack but it came to nothing.

Casemiro made no mistake when he got another chance on 56 minutes, firmly heading past Suzuki at the back post from a Gabriel cross.

Japan were reeling and Vinicius almost put Brazil ahead when he weaved his way through the area and attempted to flick over Suzuki, but the keeper just managed to touch onto the post.

Japan recovered their composure and held Brazil back for long periods in a tight ending but Brazil eventually found a way through.

Five minutes of stoppage time had elapsed when Guimaraes slipped in Martinelli and he bent a shot past Suzuki.

stats.name: wonCorners
stats.description: The number of corners won.

stats.name: yellowCards
stats.description: The number of yellow cards accumulated.

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelWinPct
stats.description: The percentage of duels won.

stats.name: turnover
stats.description: An unsuccessful touch. A player only touched the ball and lost possession – bad control

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)

stats.name: touches
stats.description: Total sum of a player's or team's on-the-ball events

stats.name: aerialsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels where the ball is in the air and possession is lost

stats.name: duelsLost
stats.description: Total number of duels over the possession of the ball where a player loses the ball - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels for possession of the ball where possession was won (aerial won + ground duels won)

stats.name: groundDuelsWon
stats.description: Total number of duels where possession is won and ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges.

stats.name: duels
stats.description: Total number of direct player-on-player confrontations for possession of the ball (aerial + ground duels)

stats.name: groundDuels
stats.description: Total number of duels over possession where ball is on the ground; including total contests, total tackles, fouls, being fouled, dispossessions and lost challenges - doesn't include 'overrun' situations where the attacking player takes on an opponent but the ball runs away from them out of play or to an opponent.

stats.name: duelWinPct
stats.description: The percentage of duels won.

stats.name: wonContest
stats.description: Total dribbles where a team player beats an opponent – no over-runs or defensive 1v1s

stats.name: totalContest
stats.description: A dribble past a player is attempted (contests always involve two players) – it does not include 'overrun' situations (where the attacking player takes on an opponent, but the ball runs away from them and then out of play or to an opponent) or 'defensive 1v1' situations (where the attacking player attempts a dribble but is stopped by the defender but without a tackle occurring)
